title: Similar effect to AT field has already been generated
display_title: Similar effect to AT field has already been generated
wikitext:
[]: Similar effect to AT field has already been generated             Quote Post by Zuggy » Tue Aug 02, 2005 2:41 pm [url]http://www.cheniere.org/books/part1/teslaweapons.htm[/url] Ah, the scientists of the 1920-60s, wonderful bunch. This could easily be thought of as an (real) AT field as it can even block gamma rays. Amazing. Maybe someone with more knowledge can comment? Oh, and look out for the word 'borderline' in the article... Interesting.

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Tue Aug 02, 2005 5:14 pm Sounds somewhat...doubtful If the Soviet Union had really been able to make this on a large scale, there would have been no need for compromise with the US.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Aug 02, 2005 7:17 pm I've heard some pretty crazy things about Tesla. Interestingly, after his death, all of his notes and research were gobbled up by various authorities and hidden away. He was a genius, and people are interested to know exactly how much of his stufff was feasible. Kinda like a Da Vinci of our time.

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Tue Aug 02, 2005 9:36 pm One thing tesla made that was proven to work was an oscillator small enough to fit in your hand. Using sonic resonance it was able to destroy things like buildings, huge iron links that even sledge hammers couldn't crush and other difficult to destroy objects simply by using sonic vibrations. If he could make something like that I wouldn't be to quick to dismiss the whole deal with the deathray, although I am a little doubtful that the soviets had perfected versions of that technology.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Aug 02, 2005 9:46 pm Defectron wrote: One thing tesla made that was proven to work was an oscillator small enough to fit in your hand. Using sonic resonance it was able to destroy things like buildings, huge iron links that even sledge hammers couldn't crush and other difficult to destroy objects simply by using sonic vibrations.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Tue Aug 02, 2005 10:33 pm Happily the shock absorbing effects of non resonant materials usually prevent most things from shaking asunder. However, resonace is such an unusual and subtle effect that it sometimes slips under the radar. Tesla was an interesting fellow. I belive one of his ideas was to transmit electric power wirelessly, using the earths longwave electro magnetic field as the medium. Don't quote me on that. One incident that springs to mind is the reported disintegration of an owl that flew too close to the extremely highly charged Wardenclyffe Tower .

[]: Other things...             Quote Post by Zuggy » Wed Aug 03, 2005 1:56 pm A couple more links... [url]http://www.totse.com/en/fringe/tesla/tesla1.html[/url] Some more information on Tesla and his works can be found here for those interested. [url]http://www.cheniere.org/books/excalibur/glossary/002edited.htm[/url] 'A-Field'? Missing the 'T' I know but still interesting. I noticed on one of the Scalar Potential Interferometer's diagrams that its sources of electricity are called 'Moray Power Units', looking into this further with Google I came across a lot of information connected with Dr Thomas Moray and his Radiant Energy Device among other free energy devices, past and present. [url]http://www.google.co.uk/search?hl=en&q=Dr+Thomas+Moray+Radiant+Energy&meta=[/url] Keeping this related to Evangelion as not incur the moderators wrath, I believe that perharps that this dipping into 'Free Energy' or 'Radiant Energy' could be the Angels S2 engine, or the base of its design. It just seems to fit. There is more information on Free Energy and the like here - [url]http://www.keelynet.com/energy/moreland.htm[/url] If he could make something like that I wouldn't be to quick to dismiss the whole deal with the deathray, although I am a little doubtful that the soviets had perfected versions of that technology.

[]:             Quote Post by TheBlueTree » Thu Aug 11, 2005 12:19 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: I've heard some pretty crazy things about Tesla. Interestingly, after his death, all of his notes and research were gobbled up by various authorities and hidden away. He was a genius, and people are interested to know exactly how much of his stufff was feasible. Kinda like a Da Vinci of our time.

[]:             Quote Post by Soluzar » Thu Aug 11, 2005 12:39 pm TheBlueTree wrote: The Eva Monkey wrote: I've heard some pretty crazy things about Tesla. Interestingly, after his death, all of his notes and research were gobbled up by various authorities and hidden away. He was a genius, and people are interested to know exactly how much of his stufff was feasible. Kinda like a Da Vinci of our time.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Thu Aug 11, 2005 2:22 pm I don't think AT-Fields actually vaporized anything; they were more like magic cellophane. Scary shit.

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Thu Aug 11, 2005 11:19 pm DaVinci created sketches for multi-barrel bows, also for some sort of the predecessor of the M1A1 tank, and probably many other weapons. Remember, he had to do something for his Mecenas, and the Lords of the Cities always were in need of weapons.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Fri Aug 12, 2005 3:22 am I've seen these weapons just in Command&Conquer :) !!!

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Fri Aug 12, 2005 3:42 pm ummm...sure as i recall, da vinci also designed flying machines that never worked an a parachute

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Wed Aug 17, 2005 12:32 pm Deleted.

[]:             Quote Post by TheBlueTree » Thu Aug 18, 2005 12:11 pm I think its impossible to creat it but w/e I guess since eva i wouldnt be to surprised to see something new lol

[]:             Quote Post by bp32 » Fri Aug 19, 2005 2:35 pm Soluzar wrote: TheBlueTree wrote: The Eva Monkey wrote: I've heard some pretty crazy things about Tesla. Interestingly, after his death, all of his notes and research were gobbled up by various authorities and hidden away. He was a genius, and people are interested to know exactly how much of his stufff was feasible. Kinda like a Da Vinci of our time.

[]:             Quote Post by Oblivious » Sat Aug 20, 2005 11:04 am When I finally build my invincible mech, it is going to have one of those tesla shields.
